footer.zonesUpdated: Conflict zones updated {date}. Decision support, not official travel advice.

consent.message: We use analytics cookies to improve FlyFast. No ads, no tracking across sites.

search.heroSubtitle: Search every flight on Google Flights. AI-powered, conflict zones filtered. Free, no login.

examples: India to Germany, next week, safe routes only
JFK to London, next week, under $500

results.roundTripExplanation: Each option shows outbound + return. Prices are combined; you'll complete two separate bookings.
results.noPricing: These routes exist but live pricing isn't available right now. Click any flight to check current prices on the provider's site.
results.roundTripDegraded: No paired round-trip options found for these dates. Showing outbound flights only; search separately for return flights.
results.partialResults: We found {count, plural, one {1 flight} other {{count} flights}} but couldn't check all dates. Some options may be missing.

risk.safeTooltip: Route avoids all known conflict zones and restricted airspace.
risk.cautionTooltip: Route passes near a lower-risk conflict area. Review details before booking.
risk.highRiskTooltip: Route crosses high-risk airspace. Consider safer alternatives.
risk.doNotFlyTooltip: Route crosses active conflict zone or restricted airspace. Strongly recommend avoiding.

noResults.timeoutExplanation: Google Flights was slow to respond. This usually works on a second try.
noResults.timeoutTip: Tip: try fewer dates, a single destination, or search at off-peak hours.
noResults.providerErrorExplanation: Flight data sources returned an error. Give it a minute and try again.
noResults.safetyFiltered: Every route crosses a conflict zone
noResults.safetyFilteredExplanation: We couldn't find a safe path. Try a nearby departure airport, different dates, or add a layover city to route around the zone.
noResults.noRoutesExplanation: This city pair doesn't have scheduled service. Try a nearby hub airport, flexible dates, or describe your trip in plain English and we'll suggest alternatives.
noResults.nothingMatchedExplanation: No flights for this route and date. Try nearby airports, shift the dates by a day or two, or relax your filters.

errors.couldNotReach: Could not reach the search API. Please try again.

rateLimit.limitReached: You've used all 10 free searches this hour. Next search available in ~{mins, plural, one {1 minute} other {{mins} minutes}}.

priceAlert.success: Price alert created. Check your email to confirm.
priceAlert.disclaimer: We'll send a confirmation email. Daily check, 90-day expiry, one-click unsubscribe.

metadata.title: FlyFast - The smartest flight search
metadata.description: Describe your trip in plain English. FlyFast searches Google Flights, filters conflict zones, and finds the safest, cheapest route. Free, no login.
metadata.ogAlt: FlyFast: describe your trip, we find the best flight
metadata.flightDescription: Found {route} from {price}. Conflict zones filtered. Search your own flights on FlyFast.

faq.a1: Type your trip in plain English, like 'Mumbai to Hamburg next week under $300'. FlyFast searches every flight on Google Flights, filters conflict zones, and ranks by price, duration, and route safety. You book directly with the airline.
faq.a2: Yes. FlyFast is completely free with no login required. You get 10 searches per hour. There are no ads and no premium tier.
faq.q3: How does FlyFast filter conflict zones?
faq.a3: FlyFast maintains a dataset of active conflict zones, restricted airspace, and regions with aviation advisories. Every flight route is checked against these zones. High-risk routes are removed from results. Caution-level routes are flagged so you can decide. The data is updated regularly.
faq.q4: Is it safe to fly over conflict zones?
faq.a4: Some airspace over conflict zones is closed or restricted by aviation authorities (NOTAMs). Airlines reroute around these areas, which can add flight time and cost. FlyFast automatically checks which routes cross conflict airspace and filters or flags them. Always check official government travel advisories before booking.
faq.q5: How do I find cheap flights that avoid conflict zones?
faq.a5: On FlyFast, type a natural language query like 'cheapest flight from London to Bangkok avoiding conflict zones'. FlyFast searches every flight on Google Flights, removes routes through high-risk airspace, and ranks the remaining options by price. You can also specify flexible dates to find the cheapest day to fly.
faq.a6: FlyFast searches every flight on Google Flights, covering all major carriers, low-cost airlines, and multi-airline itineraries worldwide.
faq.q7: Can I search for multi-city or complex routes?
faq.a7: Yes. FlyFast handles multi-city searches, flexible dates, nearby airports, and budget constraints. It searches all origin-destination-date combinations and ranks the best options.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy - FlyFast
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: FlyFast privacy policy. How we handle search data, analytics, and third-party flight providers. No personal data is sold.
privacy.intro: FlyFast is built by <link>Building Open</link>. We collect only what is necessary to operate the service and prevent abuse. We do not sell personal data.
privacy.whatWeCollectSearchQueries: <bold>Search queries</bold> -- your natural language prompts are sent to our backend to parse and search flights. We do not store your search text. Anonymized metrics (result count, duration, selected filters) are logged server-side.
privacy.whatWeCollectAnalytics: <bold>Usage analytics</bold> -- anonymized page views and interaction data via PostHog and Google Analytics, loaded only if you accept cookies. No personally identifiable information is tracked.
privacy.whatWeCollectServerAnalytics: <bold>Server-side search metrics</bold> -- result count, search duration, and selected filters are logged anonymously. Your search text is never stored.
privacy.whatWeCollectRateLimit: <bold>Rate limiting data</bold> -- your IP address is used server-side to enforce the 10 searches per hour limit. It is not stored or shared.
privacy.whatWeCollectEmail: <bold>Email for price alerts</bold> -- if you set a price alert, we store your email address and search criteria (routes, price threshold). This data is stored in a local database and used solely to send you price notifications. Alerts expire after 90 days. You can unsubscribe at any time via the link in each email.
privacy.whatWeDoNotCollect1: No account registration, no login, no passwords
privacy.whatWeDoNotCollect2: No cookies for advertising or cross-site tracking
privacy.whatWeDoNotCollect3: No payment or financial information (FlyFast does not sell tickets)
privacy.whatWeDoNotCollect4: No location data beyond what you type in your search
privacy.thirdPartyIntro: FlyFast uses the following external services to operate:
privacy.thirdPartyGoogleFlights: <bold>Google Flights</bold> -- flight data provider. Your search parameters (origins, destinations, dates) are sent to retrieve flight results.
privacy.thirdPartyGemini: <bold>Google Gemini</bold> -- AI service for parsing natural language queries into structured search parameters.
privacy.thirdPartyPostHog: <bold>PostHog</bold> -- privacy-focused analytics. EU-hosted. No personal data is sent.
privacy.thirdPartyVercel: <bold>Vercel</bold> -- hosting provider for the web application.
privacy.thirdPartyResend: <bold>Resend</bold> -- email delivery service for price alert notifications. Only your email address and alert details are shared with Resend when sending notifications.
privacy.thirdPartyOutro: Each service processes data under its own privacy policy. We recommend reviewing their terms if you have concerns.
privacy.dataRetention: Search queries are processed in real time and not stored after the response is sent. Client-side analytics data is retained for up to 12 months. Server-side search metrics are retained for up to 12 months. Rate limiting data expires automatically. Price alert data (email, search criteria) is retained for 90 days and automatically deleted after expiry.
privacy.yourRights: You can request access to, correction of, or deletion of any data we hold about you. Contact <emailLink>hello@buildingopen.org</emailLink> with your request.
privacy.cookiesContent: We use one analytics cookie from PostHog and one from Google Analytics, loaded only if you accept cookies. You can change your preference at any time via the 'Cookie settings' link in the footer. To clear cookies manually, use your browser's settings.
privacy.openSource: FlyFast is open source. You can inspect exactly what data the application collects by reviewing the code at <repoLink>github.com/buildingopen/opensky</repoLink>.

terms.title: Terms of Service - FlyFast
terms.heading: Terms of Service
terms.description: FlyFast terms of use. Service provided as-is for informational purposes. Not a substitute for official travel advisories.
terms.intro: By using FlyFast you agree to these terms. FlyFast is operated by <link>Building Open</link>.
terms.serviceBody: FlyFast is a free flight search tool that uses natural language input to search flights across Google Flights and filter routes through conflict zones. FlyFast does not sell tickets, process payments, or act as a travel agent. When you click a flight result, you are redirected to the airline or Google Flights to complete your booking.
terms.accuracyBody: Flight prices, availability, routes, and safety information can change rapidly. The information displayed on FlyFast is for informational purposes only and may differ from what you see at the point of booking. Always verify prices, schedules, and travel advisories directly with the airline and relevant government authorities.
terms.conflictBody: FlyFast provides conflict zone filtering as decision support, not as official safety guidance. The conflict zone dataset is updated regularly but may not reflect very recent developments. FlyFast is not a substitute for official NOTAMs, government travel advisories, or airline safety communications. You are responsible for verifying the safety of your route before booking.
terms.alertsBody: Price alerts are provided on a best-effort basis. Prices in alert emails are indicative and may have changed by the time you check. We are not liable for missed deals or incorrect pricing in alert notifications. Alerts are active for 90 days from creation. You can unsubscribe from any alert at any time via the link included in each email.
terms.useItem1: Do not bypass or attempt to circumvent rate limits (10 searches per hour per user).
terms.useItem2: Do not use automated scraping, bots, or other tools that degrade service availability.
terms.useItem3: Do not use FlyFast to facilitate illegal activity.
terms.useBlockNotice: We reserve the right to block access for users who violate these terms.
terms.ipBody: FlyFast is open source under the MIT license. The source code is available at <link>github.com/buildingopen/opensky</link>. Flight data is provided by Google Flights and is subject to their terms. Conflict zone data is maintained by Building Open.
terms.liabilityBody: FlyFast is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. Building Open is not liable for any losses, damages, or costs arising from your use of FlyFast, including but not limited to: inaccurate pricing, route safety misclassification, missed flights, or booking errors.
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms at any time. Changes take effect when published on this page. Continued use of FlyFast after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

imprint.title: Imprint - FlyFast
imprint.heading: Imprint
imprint.description: FlyFast legal notice (Impressum) as required by German TMG §5.
imprint.germanAuthoritative: This is a legal notice required under German law (Impressum). The German text is authoritative.
imprint.responsibleHeading: Verantwortlich für den Inhalt nach §55 Abs. 2 RStV
imprint.contentLiabilityText: Die Inhalte unserer Seiten wurden mit größter Sorgfalt erstellt. Für die Richtigkeit, Vollständigkeit und Aktualität der Inhalte können wir jedoch keine Gewähr übernehmen. Als Diensteanbieter sind wir gemäß §7 Abs. 1 TMG für eigene Inhalte auf diesen Seiten nach den allgemeinen Gesetzen verantwortlich. Nach §§8 bis 10 TMG sind wir als Diensteanbieter jedoch nicht verpflichtet, übermittelte oder gespeicherte fremde Informationen zu überwachen.
imprint.linkLiabilityText: Unser Angebot enthält Links zu externen Webseiten Dritter, auf deren Inhalte wir keinen Einfluss haben. Deshalb können wir für diese fremden Inhalte auch keine Gewähr übernehmen. Für die Inhalte der verlinkten Seiten ist stets der jeweilige Anbieter oder Betreiber der Seiten verantwortlich. Die verlinkten Seiten wurden zum Zeitpunkt der Verlinkung auf mögliche Rechtsverstöße überprüft. Rechtswidrige Inhalte waren zum Zeitpunkt der Verlinkung nicht erkennbar. Bei Bekanntwerden von Rechtsverletzungen werden wir derartige Links umgehend entfernen.
imprint.disputeTextBefore: Die Europäische Kommission stellt eine Plattform zur Online-Streitbeilegung (OS) bereit:
imprint.disputeTextAfter: Wir sind nicht bereit oder verpflichtet, an Streitbeilegungsverfahren vor einer Verbraucherschlichtungsstelle teilzunehmen.

contact.title: Contact - FlyFast
contact.heading: Contact
contact.description: Get in touch with the FlyFast team. Report bugs, ask questions, or suggest features.
contact.intro: FlyFast is built by <link>Building Open</link>, a small team focused on open source travel tools. We read every message and typically respond within 48 hours.
contact.generalBody: Email us at <email>hello@buildingopen.org</email>. Whether you have a feature idea, a question about how FlyFast works, or just want to say hello.
contact.bugsBody: Open an issue on <github>GitHub</github>. Include what you searched, what you expected, and what happened instead. Screenshots help.
contact.partnershipsBody: Travel platforms, corporate travel managers, and safety organizations: email <email>hello@buildingopen.org</email> with your use case.
contact.openSourceBody: FlyFast is fully open source. The code is at <repo>github.com/buildingopen/opensky</repo>. Contributions, pull requests, and forks are welcome. Check the README for setup instructions.
contact.faqFreeA: Yes. No login, no ads, no premium tier. You get 10 searches per hour.
contact.faqTicketsA: No. FlyFast finds and ranks flights. When you click a result, you book directly with the airline or via Google Flights.
contact.faqAccuracyQ: How accurate is the conflict zone data?
contact.faqAccuracyA: The dataset is updated regularly and covers active conflict zones, restricted airspace, and aviation advisories. It may not reflect changes from the last few days. Always check official government advisories before booking.
contact.faqApiQ: Can I use FlyFast data in my app?
contact.faqApiA: The code is open source (MIT license). For questions or collaboration, reach out at <email>hello@buildingopen.org</email>.

methodology.title: How FlyFast Works - Methodology
methodology.heading: How it works
methodology.description: How FlyFast searches every flight on Google Flights, filters conflict zones, and ranks by price, duration, and route safety.
methodology.intro: FlyFast combines natural language understanding, real-time flight data from Google Flights, and conflict zone intelligence to find the best route for your trip. This page explains exactly how.

step1.heading: 1. You describe your trip
step1.body: Type what you need in plain English. FlyFast understands origins, destinations, dates, budgets, cabin class, stop preferences, and more.
step1.example1: Mumbai to Hamburg next week, under $300
step1.example2: Cheapest flight from JFK to anywhere in Southeast Asia in July
step1.example3: Berlin to Tokyo, round trip, business class, flexible dates
step1.example4: London to New York nonstop this weekend
step1.nearbyAirports: FlyFast also expands nearby airports automatically. Searching "Hamburg" checks HAM, BRE, HAJ, LBC, and BER. Flexible dates like "cheapest in July" sample every few days across the month.

step2.heading: 2. We parse your query with AI
step2.body: Your natural language input is parsed into structured search parameters: origin airports, destination airports, date ranges, price limits, cabin class, and stop preferences. This parsing is powered by Google Gemini.
step2.tooBroad: If your query is too broad (for example, "cheapest flight anywhere"), FlyFast suggests three narrower alternatives instead of running thousands of searches.

step3.heading: 3. We search every matching flight
step3.body: FlyFast searches every origin-destination-date combination in parallel across Google Flights. For a query like "Hamburg or Berlin to Tokyo, any day next week", that could be 5 origins x 2 destinations x 7 dates = 70 searches, all running simultaneously.
step3.streaming: Results stream in as they arrive, so you see flights appearing in real time rather than waiting for all searches to finish.

step4.heading: 4. We check every route against conflict zones
step4.body: Every flight result is checked against our conflict zone dataset. We look at all airports in the itinerary and the airspace the route passes through. Routes are classified into four safety levels:
step4.safeDesc: no conflict zones in the route
step4.cautionDesc: touches a lower-risk advisory area. Shown with a warning so you can decide.
step4.highRiskDesc: passes through active conflict airspace. Removed from results.
step4.doNotFlyDesc: closed or restricted airspace. Removed from results.
step4.filtered: High-risk and do-not-fly routes are filtered out before you see them. You only see safe and caution-level flights.

step5.heading: 5. We rank and recommend
step5.body: After filtering, every remaining flight is scored across four dimensions:
step5.safetyDesc: safe routes score higher than caution routes
step5.labelsIntro: FlyFast then labels the top results:
step5.recommendedDesc: best overall balance of price, speed, comfort, and safety

step6.heading: 6. You book directly
step6.body: FlyFast does not sell tickets and does not take payment. When you click a flight, you go directly to the airline or to Google Flights where you can compare and book. Prices can change between our search and your booking.

dataSources.heading: Data sources
dataSources.flightDataDesc: Google Flights, covering major carriers, low-cost airlines, and multi-airline itineraries worldwide.
dataSources.conflictDataDesc: Maintained by Building Open. Includes active conflict zones, military activity areas, restricted airspace, and aviation advisories. Updated regularly. The "Conflict zones updated" date in the footer shows when the data was last refreshed.
dataSources.queryParsingDesc: Google Gemini (AI) for natural language understanding.

comparison.heading: FlyFast vs. traditional flight search
comparison.traditionalInput: Fill origin, destination, date, passengers forms
comparison.traditionalFlexDates: Check each day or use calendar view

limitations.heading: Limitations
limitations.notAdvice: FlyFast is decision support, not official travel advice. Always check government advisories and your airline before booking.
limitations.priceChange: Prices change in real time. The price you see may differ from the price at booking.
limitations.dataLag: Conflict zone data is updated regularly but may not reflect changes that happened in the last few days.
limitations.rateLimit: FlyFast searches are rate-limited to 10 per hour per user to prevent abuse.

safety.title: Flight Safety Map - FlyFast
safety.heading: Flight Safety
safety.subtitle: Global conflict zones and restricted airspace
safety.description: Live status of conflict zones affecting commercial aviation. Updated daily with EASA, FAA, and safeairspace.net data.
safety.dataSource: Data from EASA Conflict Zone Information Bulletins, FAA NOTAMs, and safeairspace.net. Refreshed daily.
safety.learnMore: Learn how FlyFast uses this data

riskDescriptions.do_not_fly: Airspace is closed or extremely dangerous. Airlines do not operate routes through this zone. All flights are rerouted.
riskDescriptions.high_risk: Active security threats in this airspace. Most airlines avoid this zone. Flights transiting nearby may be rerouted.
riskDescriptions.caution: Advisory-level concern. Airports in this zone operate normally, but nearby conflict zones may affect some overflights. Airlines monitor the situation and reroute if needed.

travelerAdvice.do_not_fly: No commercial flights operate through this airspace. If your route would normally transit this zone, airlines automatically reroute around it. You do not need to take any action, but flights may be longer or more expensive due to detours. FlyFast filters out any routes that would pass through this zone.
travelerAdvice.high_risk: Most airlines avoid this airspace. FlyFast flags any flights that transit nearby so you can choose safer alternatives. Check your airline's route policy if you are flying to a neighboring country.
travelerAdvice.caution: Airports in or near this zone operate normally. You may see a Caution badge on flights transiting this area. This means the route passes near a conflict zone but is not directly affected. Monitor travel advisories from your government before booking.

zone.dataRefreshed: Data refreshed daily from EASA, FAA, and safeairspace.net.
zone.safeToFlyTitle: Is it safe to fly over {zone}? - FlyFast
zone.faqQuestion: Is it safe to fly over {zone}?
zone.faqAnswer_do_not_fly: No. {zone} airspace is classified as Do Not Fly. {details} Airlines automatically reroute flights around this zone.
zone.faqAnswer_high_risk: {zone} is classified as High Risk. {details} Most airlines avoid this airspace.
zone.faqAnswer_caution: {zone} is classified as Caution. {details} Airports operate normally but flights may be rerouted near conflict zones.

alerts.title: Get notified when risk levels change
alerts.subtitle: Select zones to monitor. We'll email you when their status changes.
